revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster]][[ja:見島ジーコンボ古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11630178]] Wikidata name: Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun cluster in Yamaguchi, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster | native_name =見島ジーコンボ古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Jīkombo Kofun Group.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|45|49|N|131|09|34|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|34.763611|N|131.159444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Hagi, Yamaguchi|Hagi, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|45|49|N|131|09|34|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster'''|見島ジーコンボ古墳群|}} is a group of [[kofun| Kofun]]s, located on the island of [[:en:Mishima Island, Yamaguchi|Mishima]] in the city of [[:en:Hagi, Yamaguchi|Hagi, Yamaguchi]] in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The grave cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958, with the area under protection expanded in 1984.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2443|title=見島ジーコンボ古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 22, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mishima Jiikonbo group of Kofuns is located on a gravel beach on the southeastern coast of Mishima Island, which located in the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], about 45 kilometers off the coast of Hagi. Approximately 200 ancient Kofuns are densely built on the Yokoura coastline from Mt. Takami to Mt. Bandai in Honmura, with a width of 50 to 100 meters extending for about 300 meters from east to west. All these Kofuns were made by piling up natural [[:en:Basalt|Basalt]] stones from gravel beaches without the use of any soil. About 10% of the total number of Kofuns has been excavated, and have bene found to contain a horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a short passage and a wide, shallow box-type sarcophagus-like burial facility. These Kofuns are believed to date from the 8th to the 9th centuries, based on a number of coins which have been found with [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated items include weapons such as iron swords and arrowheads, accessories such as gilt-bronze hairpins and small glass beads, earthenware such as green-glazed pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], bronze products such as copper bells and copper mirrors. Because the combination of stacked stone mounds and burial goods is common to many [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] mounds in [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], and the characteristics of the burial accessories is rare for western Japan, and also considering the small size of the settlements on the island at the time, it is speculated that these are the graves of [[:en:kondei|kondei]], conscripted militia, who were dispatched to the island, possible from northern Japan, due to military tensions with Korea. the excavated items are in the Hagi Museum and the Yamaguchi Prefectural Archaeological Center. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=100016&pid=bl Yamaguchi Prefecture Tourism, Sports and Culture Department Culture Promotion Division]{{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:Completion tier 2]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster]][[ja:見島ジーコンボ古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11630178]] Wikidata name: Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun cluster in Yamaguchi, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster | native_name =見島ジーコンボ古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Jīkombo Kofun Group.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|45|49|N|131|09|34|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|34.763611|N|131.159444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Hagi, Yamaguchi|Hagi, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|45|49|N|131|09|34|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster'''|見島ジーコンボ古墳群|}} is a group of [[kofun| Kofun]]s, located on the island of [[:en:Mishima Island, Yamaguchi|Mishima]] in the city of [[:en:Hagi, Yamaguchi|Hagi, Yamaguchi]] in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The grave cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958, with the area under protection expanded in 1984.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2443|title=見島ジーコンボ古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 22, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mishima Jiikonbo group of Kofuns is located on a gravel beach on the southeastern coast of Mishima Island, which located in the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], about 45 kilometers off the coast of Hagi. Approximately 200 ancient Kofuns are densely built on the Yokoura coastline from Mt. Takami to Mt. Bandai in Honmura, with a width of 50 to 100 meters extending for about 300 meters from east to west. All these Kofuns were made by piling up natural [[:en:Basalt|Basalt]] stones from gravel beaches without the use of any soil. About 10% of the total number of Kofuns has been excavated, and have bene found to contain a horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a short passage and a wide, shallow box-type sarcophagus-like burial facility. These Kofuns are believed to date from the 8th to the 9th centuries, based on a number of coins which have been found with [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated items include weapons such as iron swords and arrowheads, accessories such as gilt-bronze hairpins and small glass beads, earthenware such as green-glazed pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], bronze products such as copper bells and copper mirrors. Because the combination of stacked stone mounds and burial goods is common to many [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] mounds in [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], and the characteristics of the burial accessories is rare for western Japan, and also considering the small size of the settlements on the island at the time, it is speculated that these are the graves of [[:en:kondei|kondei]], conscripted militia, who were dispatched to the island, possible from northern Japan, due to military tensions with Korea. the excavated items are in the Hagi Museum and the Yamaguchi Prefectural Archaeological Center. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=100016&pid=bl Yamaguchi Prefecture Tourism, Sports and Culture Department Culture Promotion Division]{{in lang|ja}}

Because the combination of stacked stone mounds and burial goods is common to many [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] mounds in [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], and the characteristics of the burial accessories is rare for western Japan, and also considering the small size of the settlements on the island at the time, it is speculated that these are the graves of [[:en:kondei|kondei]], conscripted militia, who were dispatched to the island, possible from northern Japan, due to military tensions with Korea. the excavated items are in the Hagi Museum and the Yamaguchi Prefectural Archaeological Center. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster]][[ja:見島ジーコンボ古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11630178]] Wikidata name: Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun cluster in Yamaguchi, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster | native_name =見島ジーコンボ古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Jīkombo Kofun Group.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|45|49|N|131|09|34|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|34.763611|N|131.159444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Hagi, Yamaguchi|Hagi, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th-8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|45|49|N|131|09|34|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster'''|見島ジーコンボ古墳群|}} is a group of [[kofun| Kofun]]s, located on the island of [[:en:Mishima Island, Yamaguchi|Mishima]] in the city of [[:en:Hagi, Yamaguchi|Hagi, Yamaguchi]] in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The grave cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958, with the area under protection expanded in 1984.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2443|title=見島ジーコンボ古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 22, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mishima Jiikonbo group of Kofuns is located on a gravel beach on the southeastern coast of Mishima Island, which located in the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], about 45 kilometers off the coast of Hagi. Approximately 200 ancient Kofuns are densely built on the Yokoura coastline from Mt. Takami to Mt. Bandai in Honmura, with a width of 50 to 100 meters extending for about 300 meters from east to west. All these Kofuns were made by piling up natural [[:en:Basalt|Basalt]] stones from gravel beaches without the use of any soil. About 10% of the total number of Kofuns has been excavated, and have bene found to contain a horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a short passage and a wide, shallow box-type sarcophagus-like burial facility. These Kofuns are believed to date from the 8th to the 9th centuries, based on a number of coins which have been found with [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated items include weapons such as iron swords and arrowheads, accessories such as gilt-bronze hairpins and small glass beads, earthenware such as green-glazed pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], bronze products such as copper bells and copper mirrors. Because the combination of stacked stone mounds and burial goods is common to many [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] mounds in [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], and the characteristics of the burial accessories is rare for western Japan, and also considering the small size of the settlements on the island at the time, it is speculated that these are the graves of [[:en:kondei|kondei]], conscripted militia, who were dispatched to the island, possible from northern Japan, due to military tensions with Korea. the excavated items are in the Hagi Museum and the Yamaguchi Prefectural Archaeological Center. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=100016&pid=bl Yamaguchi Prefecture Tourism, Sports and Culture Department Culture Promotion Division]{{in lang|ja}}

All these Kofuns were made by piling up natural [[:en:Basalt|Basalt]] stones from gravel beaches without the use of any soil. About 10% of the total number of Kofuns has been excavated, and have bene found to contain a horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a short passage and a wide, shallow box-type sarcophagus-like burial facility. These Kofuns are believed to date from the 8th to the 9th centuries, based on a number of coins which have been found with [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated items include weapons such as iron swords and arrowheads, accessories such as gilt-bronze hairpins and small glass beads, earthenware such as green-glazed pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], bronze products such as copper bells and copper mirrors. Because the combination of stacked stone mounds and burial goods is common to many [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] mounds in [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], and the characteristics of the burial accessories is rare for western Japan, and also considering the small size of the settlements on the island at the time, it is speculated that these are the graves of [[:en:kondei|kondei]], conscripted militia, who were dispatched to the island, possible from northern Japan, due to military tensions with Korea. the excavated items are in the Hagi Museum and the Yamaguchi Prefectural Archaeological Center. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster'''|見島ジーコンボ古墳群|}} is a group of [[kofun| Kofun]]s, located on the island of [[:en:Mishima Island, Yamaguchi|Mishima]] in the city of [[:en:Hagi, Yamaguchi|Hagi, Yamaguchi]] in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The grave cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958, with the area under protection expanded in 1984.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2443|title=見島ジーコンボ古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 22, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mishima Jiikonbo group of Kofuns is located on a gravel beach on the southeastern coast of Mishima Island, which located in the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], about 45 kilometers off the coast of Hagi. Approximately 200 ancient Kofuns are densely built on the Yokoura coastline from Mt. Takami to Mt. Bandai in Honmura, with a width of 50 to 100 meters extending for about 300 meters from east to west. All these Kofuns were made by piling up natural [[:en:Basalt|Basalt]] stones from gravel beaches without the use of any soil. About 10% of the total number of Kofuns has been excavated, and have bene found to contain a horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a short passage and a wide, shallow box-type sarcophagus-like burial facility. These Kofuns are believed to date from the 8th to the 9th centuries, based on a number of coins which have been found with [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated items include weapons such as iron swords and arrowheads, accessories such as gilt-bronze hairpins and small glass beads, earthenware such as green-glazed pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], bronze products such as copper bells and copper mirrors. Because the combination of stacked stone mounds and burial goods is common to many [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] mounds in [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], and the characteristics of the burial accessories is rare for western Japan, and also considering the small size of the settlements on the island at the time, it is speculated that these are the graves of [[:en:kondei|kondei]], conscripted militia, who were dispatched to the island, possible from northern Japan, due to military tensions with Korea. the excavated items are in the Hagi Museum and the Yamaguchi Prefectural Archaeological Center. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=100016&pid=bl Yamaguchi Prefecture Tourism, Sports and Culture Department Culture Promotion Division]{{in lang|ja}}

Approximately 200 ancient Kofuns are densely built on the Yokoura coastline from Mt. Takami to Mt. Bandai in Honmura, with a width of 50 to 100 meters extending for about 300 meters from east to west. All these Kofuns were made by piling up natural [[:en:Basalt|Basalt]] stones from gravel beaches without the use of any soil. About 10% of the total number of Kofuns has been excavated, and have bene found to contain a horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a short passage and a wide, shallow box-type sarcophagus-like burial facility. These Kofuns are believed to date from the 8th to the 9th centuries, based on a number of coins which have been found with [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated items include weapons such as iron swords and arrowheads, accessories such as gilt-bronze hairpins and small glass beads, earthenware such as green-glazed pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], bronze products such as copper bells and copper mirrors. Because the combination of stacked stone mounds and burial goods is common to many [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] mounds in [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], and the characteristics of the burial accessories is rare for western Japan, and also considering the small size of the settlements on the island at the time, it is speculated that these are the graves of [[:en:kondei|kondei]], conscripted militia, who were dispatched to the island, possible from northern Japan, due to military tensions with Korea. the excavated items are in the Hagi Museum and the Yamaguchi Prefectural Archaeological Center. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=100016&pid=bl Yamaguchi Prefecture Tourism, Sports and Culture Department Culture Promotion Division]{{in lang|ja}}

Because the combination of stacked stone mounds and burial goods is common to many {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}} mounds in {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}, and the characteristics of the burial accessories is rare for western Japan, and also considering the small size of the settlements on the island at the time, it is speculated that these are the graves of {{ill|kondei|en|kondei|WD=Q4230683|lt=kondei}}, conscripted militia, who were dispatched to the island, possible from northern Japan, due to military tensions with Korea. the excavated items are in the Hagi Museum and the Yamaguchi Prefectural Archaeological Center. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster'''|見島ジーコンボ古墳群|}} is a group of [[kofun| Kofun]]s, located on the island of {{ill|Mishima Island, Yamaguchi|en|Mishima Island, Yamaguchi|WD=Q49513|lt=Mishima}} in the city of {{ill|Hagi, Yamaguchi|en|Hagi, Yamaguchi|WD=Q378225|lt=Hagi, Yamaguchi}} in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The grave cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958, with the area under protection expanded in 1984.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2443|title=見島ジーコンボ古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 22, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mishima Jiikonbo group of Kofuns is located on a gravel beach on the southeastern coast of Mishima Island, which located in the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}}, about 45 kilometers off the coast of Hagi. Approximately 200 ancient Kofuns are densely built on the Yokoura coastline from Mt. Takami to Mt. Bandai in Honmura, with a width of 50 to 100 meters extending for about 300 meters from east to west. All these Kofuns were made by piling up natural {{ill|Basalt|en|Basalt|WD=Q43338|lt=Basalt}} stones from gravel beaches without the use of any soil. About 10% of the total number of Kofuns has been excavated, and have bene found to contain a horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a short passage and a wide, shallow box-type sarcophagus-like burial facility. These Kofuns are believed to date from the 8th to the 9th centuries, based on a number of coins which have been found with {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated items include weapons such as iron swords and arrowheads, accessories such as gilt-bronze hairpins and small glass beads, earthenware such as green-glazed pottery and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}, bronze products such as copper bells and copper mirrors. Because the combination of stacked stone mounds and burial goods is common to many {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}} mounds in {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}, and the characteristics of the burial accessories is rare for western Japan, and also considering the small size of the settlements on the island at the time, it is speculated that these are the graves of {{ill|kondei|en|kondei|WD=Q4230683|lt=kondei}}, conscripted militia, who were dispatched to the island, possible from northern Japan, due to military tensions with Korea. the excavated items are in the Hagi Museum and the Yamaguchi Prefectural Archaeological Center. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

Because the combination of stacked stone mounds and burial goods is common to many {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}} mounds in {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}, and the characteristics of the burial accessories is rare for western Japan, and also considering the small size of the settlements on the island at the time, it is speculated that these are the graves of {{ill|kondei|en|kondei|WD=Q4230683|lt=kondei}}, conscripted militia, who were dispatched to the island, possible from northern Japan, due to military tensions with Korea. the excavated items are in the Hagi Museum and the Yamaguchi Prefectural Archaeological Center. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)|WD=Q6573292|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)}}

The {{nihongo|'''Mishima Jiikonbo Kofun Cluster'''|見島ジーコンボ古墳群|}} is a group of [[kofun| Kofun]]s, located on the island of {{ill|Mishima Island, Yamaguchi|en|Mishima Island, Yamaguchi|WD=Q49513|lt=Mishima}} in the city of {{ill|Hagi, Yamaguchi|en|Hagi, Yamaguchi|WD=Q378225|lt=Hagi, Yamaguchi}} in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The grave cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1958, with the area under protection expanded in 1984.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2443|title=見島ジーコンボ古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 22, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mishima Jiikonbo group of Kofuns is located on a gravel beach on the southeastern coast of Mishima Island, which located in the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}}, about 45 kilometers off the coast of Hagi. Approximately 200 ancient Kofuns are densely built on the Yokoura coastline from Mt. Takami to Mt. Bandai in Honmura, with a width of 50 to 100 meters extending for about 300 meters from east to west. All these Kofuns were made by piling up natural {{ill|Basalt|en|Basalt|WD=Q43338|lt=Basalt}} stones from gravel beaches without the use of any soil. About 10% of the total number of Kofuns has been excavated, and have bene found to contain a horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a short passage and a wide, shallow box-type sarcophagus-like burial facility. These Kofuns are believed to date from the 8th to the 9th centuries, based on a number of coins which have been found with {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

Because the combination of stacked stone mounds and burial goods is common to many {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}} mounds in {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}, and the characteristics of the burial accessories is rare for western Japan, and also considering the small size of the settlements on the island at the time, it is speculated that these are the graves of {{ill|kondei|en|kondei|WD=Q4230683|lt=kondei}}, conscripted militia, who were dispatched to the island, possible from northern Japan, due to military tensions with Korea. the excavated items are in the Hagi Museum and the Yamaguchi Prefectural Archaeological Center. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

Excavated items include weapons such as iron swords and arrowheads, accessories such as gilt-bronze hairpins and small glass beads, earthenware such as green-glazed pottery and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, bronze products such as copper bells and copper mirrors. Because the combination of stacked stone mounds and burial goods is common to many {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}} mounds in {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}, and the characteristics of the burial accessories is rare for western Japan, and also considering the small size of the settlements on the island at the time, it is speculated that these are the graves of {{ill|kondei|en|kondei|WD=Q4230683|lt=kondei|1=Kondei}}, conscripted militia, who were dispatched to the island, possible from northern Japan, due to military tensions with Korea. the excavated items are in the Hagi Museum and the Yamaguchi Prefectural Archaeological Center. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)|WD=Q6573292|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)}}
